reasoning: The threshold (current value as of latest release for Jun 7) is 1,935,000. This is exceptionally high relative to the recent trend (the linear trend value is ~1,902,000, and standard deviation is ~22,000). Because 1.935M is a peak roughly 1.5 standard deviations above the trend line, short-term mean reversion downward is highly likely, pushing P(increase) low for the closest dates (e.g., Jun 15 and Jul 8). The series historically exhibits a slightly increasing long-term drift (roughly +90k per year, according to the linear model slope of 238 per day). Over a 3 to 6 month horizon (Sep and Dec 2025), the underlying trendline catches up to and eventually slightly exceeds the 1.935M threshold. As the forecast horizon lengthens, the natural variance around the trend increases uncertainty, naturally pushing probabilities closer to 0.5. Hence, probabilities steadily regress toward (and slightly past) 50% for to the furthest date.
